title: Feature Flags
description: Decouple deploy from release; safer experiments; needs kill switches and governance.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Feature flag infrastructure costs vary by approach. Min=$0 represents self-hosted open-source solutions (Unleash, Flagsmith) using existing infrastructure with minimal overhead (<1% runtime impact). Max=$500 covers commercial SaaS solutions like LaunchDarkly Basic ($35-75/seat for small teams), Split.io Starter ($33/seat), or ConfigCat Pro ($75/month), plus potential infrastructure for flag evaluation caching and metrics collection. Enterprise SaaS plans (LaunchDarkly Enterprise, Split Enterprise) can exceed $1000/month but represent outlier scenarios. Most teams start with free tiers (LaunchDarkly's 1000 MAUs free, Flagsmith self-hosted) or low-cost managed solutions.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity adoption due to multiple factors: (1) Technical implementation - SDK integration across services, flag evaluation logic, fallback mechanisms, and kill switches (20-40 hours); (2) Governance framework - establishing flag lifecycle policies, naming conventions, cleanup procedures, and ownership model (10-20 hours); (3) Cultural shift - training teams on deployment vs. release separation, gradual rollout strategies, and experimentation workflows (10-15 hours); (4) Testing strategy changes - adapting CI/CD for flag-aware testing and ensuring all flag states are covered (10-15 hours). With AI coding assistants, technical implementation accelerates 30-40%, but governance and process design remain people-intensive. Total estimate: 50-90 engineering hours at $40-50/hour blended rate = $2000-4500, using $2000 as conservative mid-point.
provenance.licenseCost: Starting cost assumes open-source approach. Multiple production-grade OSS options available: Unleash (Apache 2.0), Flagsmith (BSD-3), GrowthBook (MIT), or Flipper (MIT for Ruby). Cloud providers also offer native solutions included in existing licensing (AWS AppConfig, Azure App Configuration, GCP Firebase Remote Config). Commercial SaaS becomes cost-effective at scale but isn't required for adoption. Organizations can start with $0 license cost and migrate to commercial solutions ($1000-5000/year) only if advanced features (audit logs, multi-environment management, A/B testing analytics, role-based permissions) justify the investment. Setting baseline at $0 reflects achievable zero-license-cost path.

provides.reasoning: Feature flags enable runtime toggling of features without redeployment, providing core functionality to control feature visibility through configurable flags.
Feature flags enable progressive rollouts including canary releases, percentage-based rollouts, and A/B testing by controlling feature visibility to specific user segments.
Core pattern purpose is to decouple code deployment from feature release, allowing dark launches and safer rollbacks by separating when code ships from when features activate.
Release feature flags IS the feature flags pattern — it provides runtime flag evaluation, flag lifecycle management, and progressive rollout control.

requires.reasoning: Feature flags require persistent storage and management of feature state, rules, and targeting criteria that can be updated without code changes.
The system must evaluate flag states at runtime to determine which features are active for specific requests or users based on configured rules.
Pattern requires monitoring to support kill switches mentioned in the description, enabling detection of issues and emergency feature disablement.
Governance requirements necessitate controlled access to flag management to prevent unauthorized feature toggles and ensure proper approval workflows.

supports_nfr.reason: Feature flag service introduces a dependency point that caps availability at four nines.
Feature flag evaluation adds 1-5ms latency overhead for flag lookup and evaluation.
Feature flag evaluation at p99 can reach 2-10ms due to flag store latency spikes.
Feature flags enable instant rollback by toggling flags in approximately 1 second.
Pattern supports high throughput of 1000 evaluations/second (3.6M/hour) with local caching.
Pattern is GDPR-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is CCPA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is HIPAA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling and provider BAAs, not this pattern.
Pattern is SOX-agnostic; compliance depends on audit controls in the application layer, not this pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Feature flags are a cloud-agnostic deployment pattern implementable on any infrastructure.
Feature flags are a deployment pattern with SDK support available for all mainstream languages.
Feature flags apply to any platform that executes application code and requires runtime configuration.
Feature flags control feature visibility in both synchronous and asynchronous messaging systems.
Feature flags enable controlled rollout of AI/ML features but do not require AI inference.
Feature flags support tenant-specific configurations in multi-tenant systems but work in single-tenant apps too.
Feature flags can control batch job behavior and are compatible with both batch and real-time processing.
Feature flags are orthogonal to transaction coordination and work with or without distributed transactions.
Feature flags can control streaming feature behavior and are compatible with both streaming and non-streaming systems.
Feature flags can control vector search feature rollout but do not require vector search capability.

flag_storage.description: Where feature flag states are stored. Config files are simple but require redeployment; databases allow runtime changes; feature flag services (LaunchDarkly, Split.io) provide advanced targeting and analytics; environment variables are simplest for basic flags.

evaluation_strategy.description: How flags determine which users see features. Simple boolean is on/off for all; percentage rollout enables gradual releases; user targeting allows specific user/group rules; multi-variate supports A/B/n testing with multiple variants.

targeting_capability.description: Granularity of feature targeting. None means all-or-nothing; user attributes allow targeting by user properties; context-based includes environment/location/device; advanced segments support complex rule combinations.

kill_switch_enabled.description: Whether flags can be used as emergency kill switches to instantly disable problematic features in production without redeployment. Critical for risk mitigation.

audit_logging.description: Track all flag changes and evaluations for compliance and debugging. Adds overhead but provides governance and troubleshooting capabilities.
